review2: A dockside gem with seafood that couldn't be fresher. Nicos Pier 38 sources locally when possible and the fish and chips are light, crispy, and generously portioned.
review3: The seafood chowder at Nicos Pier 38 is thick, briny, and packed with chunks of fish — one of the best bowls I've had in Hawaii. The poke add-on is also a great value.

review2: Fast, fresh, and affordable — Marugame Udon is a genius concept. You customize your bowl at the counter and it's ready in minutes without sacrificing quality.
review3: Love this place for a quick lunch. The udon noodles at Marugame Udon are made fresh daily and you can really taste the difference. The inari tofu pouches are a must.

review2: A local institution that never disappoints. Rainbow Drive In is no-frills, cash-only, and absolutely worth it — the kalua pig is smoky and tender like it came straight from the imu.
review3: Pure aloha on a plate. Rainbow Drive In is the kind of place locals bring visitors to show them what real Hawaiian food tastes like. The haupia for dessert is a must.

review3: The tom kha soup at Thai Village is warming and fragrant, exactly what you want on a rainy Oahu day. Service is friendly and the prices are very fair.

review2: Pho King is my go-to for a quick, satisfying Vietnamese meal. The banh mi is crispy outside and packed with flavor inside, and the prices are very reasonable.
review3: Good spot for Vietnamese comfort food. The portions at Pho King are generous and the spring rolls are always fresh. Service can be a bit slow but worth the wait.

name: Totoya
review1: A fantastic dining experience at Totoya — the food was fresh, the service was warm, and the atmosphere had that unmistakable island charm. We'll definitely be back.
review2: Solid neighborhood spot that always delivers. Totoya is consistent, reasonably priced, and the staff genuinely cares about making sure you leave happy.
review3: One of our regular spots in Hawaii. Totoya keeps the quality high and never feels like it's phoning it in — every visit feels like eating somewhere that takes pride in its food.

name: Sub Shack
review1: Fresh, chunky poke that rivals what you'd find at any fish market. Sub Shack doesn't skimp on the ahi and the spicy mayo sauce is addictive.
review2: Quick, affordable, and consistently good. Sub Shack is my lunch spot when I want something light but satisfying — the garlic shrimp bowl is also a hidden gem.
review3: The poke at Sub Shack is beautifully seasoned and never watery. Love that you can customize your toppings; I always go with limu, green onion, and sesame oil.

name: Hi Sliders
review1: Juicy, hand-smashed patties with fresh local toppings — Hi Sliders makes a burger that's hard to beat anywhere in Hawaii. The fries are thick-cut and perfectly salted.
review2: Hi Sliders is the kind of no-frills burger joint that delivers exactly what it promises. The double cheeseburger is stacked high and the shakes are thick and creamy.
review3: Great local burger spot that uses quality beef and doesn't overcharge. Hi Sliders is a step above the chain spots and always hits the spot after a beach day.

name: Chiefs Luau

review3: One of our favorite date night spots. My Ohanas Bbq has a lively atmosphere and the wagyu upgrade is worth every penny. Book ahead because it fills up fast on weekends.
